howItWorks.intro: This deadlift max calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorks.outro: Individual results vary based on metabolism, genetics, and activity level. Consult your doctor before starting any program.

faq.question: What is a good deadlift?
faq.answer: For men: 2× body weight is intermediate, 2.5× is advanced, 3× is elite. The deadlift is typically the heaviest lift for most people.

faq.question: Conventional vs sumo?
faq.answer: Both are valid competition lifts. Sumo uses a wider stance and shorter range of motion. Neither is inherently better — try both and use what feels strongest.

faq.question: How do I avoid injury?
faq.answer: Maintain a neutral spine, brace your core, keep the bar close to your body, and do not round your lower back. Start light and perfect your form.

faq.question: How often should I deadlift?
faq.answer: 1-2 times per week is optimal for most people. The deadlift is very taxing on the nervous system and requires adequate recovery.

howWeCalculate.disclaimer: These calculations are estimates based on standard formulas. For critical decisions, always consult a qualified professional.
